problem: Resolve Yuri's QA findings across PRs #14-17 before merge. Critical issue: validation ranges diverge across frontend, backend Pydantic, and DB CHECK constraints. Need one authoritative set of ranges, a merge order, and action items per engineer.
context: Yuri found 17 findings (4 HIGH, 8 MEDIUM, 5 LOW). The cross-boundary range conflict for creatinine (Pydantic 0.1-25.0 vs DB 0.3-15.0) means valid API requests would silently fail on DB insert. BUN max is 100 (frontend/fixtures) vs 150 (DB/main). Hemoglobin and glucose also misaligned. PR #15 was branched from stale main and would regress PredictRequest if merged. Yuri recommends merge order: PR16 → PR15 (rebased) → PR14 → PR17.

agents.name: Luca
agents.framing: You own the architecture decisions and merge authority. Your job is to drive consensus, make binding decisions on range values, and ensure the team leaves with a clear action plan. You care about system integrity — no silent failures across boundaries.

agents.name: John Donaldson
agents.framing: You own the FastAPI backend, Pydantic models, and the /predict endpoint. You understand the CKD-EPI formula's input requirements and what ranges are clinically meaningful. You also need to rebase PR #15. You care about API correctness and not accepting values the DB will reject.

agents.name: Gay Mark
agents.framing: You own the PostgreSQL schema, CHECK constraints, and the leads table. You know exactly what the DB accepts and rejects. You care about data integrity — the DB is the last line of defense. You also understand the clinical ranges from the original schema design.
